title: linaria supina var. pruinosa
intro: Linaria supina var. pruinosa, also known as Purple Toadflax, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Snapdragon family (Scrophulariaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties that help relieve congestion. The health benefits of Linaria supina var. pruinosa include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural antiseptic. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional folk medicine to promote overall well-being.
